items.text: Campus Players Present: The Night Market! Friday, May 15, 7:00 PM at Anderson Hall. Tickets $5 at the door. Student ID required. Proceeds support next semester’s production. Come enjoy an evening of drama and mystery!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is required to buy a ticket?
questions.explanation: The flyer states 'Student ID required' for ticket purchase.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, when does the performance take place?
questions.explanation: The flyer states 'Friday, May 15' as the performance date.

items.text: The campus garden is open for spring! Visit Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM near the Science Building. Bring your own gloves if you want to help plant herbs. I love the fresh smell there! 🌱

questions.stem: According to the social media post, what should visitors bring if they want to help?

options.A: To borrow tools from the shed
options.B: To purchase seeds from the office
options.C: To bring their own gardening gloves

questions.explanation: The post says 'Bring your own gloves if you want to help plant herbs.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the campus garden?

options.A: It is likely popular among students who enjoy nature.
options.B: It is open only on weekends during spring.
options.C: It charges a fee for entering the garden area.
options.D: It requires advance registration for volunteers.

questions.explanation: The author expresses enjoyment ('I love the fresh smell there!') and invites help, suggesting it is a pleasant place that attracts nature-loving students.

items.text:
Hi Jordan,

This email confirms your volunteer shift for the campus tour on this Friday, March 10, from 2:00-4:00 PM. Please meet at the Admissions Office in Anderson Hall. Wear comfortable shoes.

Best,
Ms. Chen

questions.stem: According to the email, where should Jordan meet for the shift?

options.C: At the Admissions Office in Anderson Hall
options.D: At the campus garden near Science Building

questions.explanation: The email states 'Please meet at the Admissions Office in Anderson Hall.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Jordan's role?

options.A: Jordan is expected to give a presentation.
options.B: Jordan will be walking around campus giving tours.
options.C: Jordan must bring a group of students to the office.
options.D: Jordan needs to prepare a welcome speech.

questions.explanation: The advice to 'Wear comfortable shoes' and the tour context strongly suggest Jordan will be walking to show visitors around campus.

items.text: Free Flu Shot Clinic! Tuesday, October 20, 10:00 AM-2:00 PM at the Student Health Center. No appointment needed. Free for all students with valid ID. Limited supply – first come, first served. Protect yourself this season!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is the location of the flu shot clinic?
questions.explanation: The flyer states 'at the Student Health Center' as the location.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the flu shot clinic?

options.A: Students should arrive early to ensure receiving a shot.
options.B: Students must register online before attending the clinic.
options.C: The clinic will charge a small fee for the vaccination.
options.D: The clinic is only open to students with health insurance.

questions.explanation: The phrase 'first come, first served' and 'Limited supply' imply that arriving early is advisable to secure a flu shot.
